elements.label: A first remote terminal receives an address and event data and correlates the address to location-based data using a database.

elements.label: A second terminal includes a map display and receives location-based data from a location system based on the second terminal's own location.

elements.label: The first terminal stores the second terminal's location-based data in memory.

urls.title: Geocode an address | Geocoding API
urls.snippet: Geocoding returns a GeocodeAddressResponse object that contains the results array of GeocodeResult objects. Each GeocodeResult object represents a single place.
urls.rationale: Official Geocoding API guide explicitly says geocoding returns a response that maps an address to a LatLng, directly supporting address-to-location correlation using Google Maps data.

urls.title: Method: geocode.address.geocodeAddress
urls.snippet: This method performs an address geocode, which maps an address to a LatLng. It also provides structured information about the address.
urls.rationale: Reference doc states the method performs an address geocode that maps an address to a LatLng and provides structured address information.

urls.title: REST Resource: geocode.address
urls.snippet: This method performs an address geocode, which maps an address to a LatLng. geocodeAddressQuery. This method performs an address geocode, which ...
urls.rationale: REST resource documentation for geocode.address directly describes mapping an address to LatLng, which is strong evidence for the claim's address lookup/correlation limitation.

urls.title: Location data | Maps SDK for iOS
urls.snippet: The location data available to an iOS device includes the current location ... The following tutorial and code sample demonstrate the use of location on a map:.
urls.rationale: Official iOS Maps SDK location-data page; describes device current location, using location on a map, and related map display behavior. Strong evidence for receiving device-based location data, handling it in the app, and showing it on a map.

urls.title: Locator Plus implementation guide
urls.snippet: You can geocode your addresses on the server side, store the latitudes and longitudes in your database, and refresh at least every 30 days. Here is an example ...
urls.rationale: Official Locator Plus implementation guide describes geocoding addresses on the server side, storing latitudes/longitudes in a database, identifying user location, sending directions to mobile devices, and displaying store locations on a map. This is the closest overall match to the claimed dispatch/location workflow.

urls.title: Place Autocomplete Data API | Maps JavaScript API
urls.snippet: The Place Autocomplete Data API lets you fetch place predictions programmatically, to create customized autocomplete experiences with a finer degree of control.
urls.rationale: Official Maps JavaScript API docs for Place Autocomplete Data directly describe fetching place predictions and creating autocomplete experiences, which is strong evidence for address lookup/geocoding-style correlation and automatic entry of a selected place/address into a map UI.

urls.title: Google Maps Platform Service Specific Terms
urls.snippet: Customer may use Google Maps Content from the Geocoding API in Customer Applications without a corresponding Google Map. 5.2 No use with a non-Google map.
urls.rationale: Official service terms explicitly mention Geocoding API, caching, and use of Google Maps content in customer applications; useful supporting evidence for geocoding/address lookup and related data handling/dispatch workflow.

urls.title: Special delivery with Google Maps APIs
urls.snippet: Using Google Maps, honestbee created a web-based map that shows the locations of drivers and their pickup and dropoff destinations. The company ...
urls.rationale: Official Google Cloud blog case study describing dispatchers using Google Maps APIs to match drivers with orders/customers, show driver locations on a web-based map, and automate dispatching. Strongly supports map display plus criteria-based dispatch behavior.

urls.title: Select Current Place and Show Details on a Map
urls.snippet: This tutorial demonstrates building an iOS app that retrieves the device's current location, identifies likely locations, prompts the user to select the best ...
urls.rationale: Tutorial for retrieving the device's current location, identifying likely places, and showing details on a map. Closely matches device-location input and automatic map display of a selected place.

urls.title: Geolocation API overview
urls.snippet: It returns latitude/longitude coordinates and a radius indicating the accuracy of the result for each valid input. Note: To get device location ...
urls.rationale: Official Geolocation API overview explains determining a device's latitude/longitude from cell tower and Wi‑Fi data and sending device data to the API, which supports device-location acquisition and transmission/storage aspects of the claim.
